Variant Summary: Two pathogenic PMS2 variants at high VAF (48-55%) indicate Lynch syndrome/MMR deficiency, alongside oncogenic TP53 and RB1 frameshifts suggesting an aggressive tumor with potential for immunotherapy response. The ESR1 hotspot mutation (p.Y537S) at low VAF indicates hormone therapy resistance emerging in this tumor.

Two pathogenic/likely pathogenic variants in COQ8A (VAF 46.4%) and SKIV2L (VAF 48.0%) suggest potential germline mutations associated with rare genetic disorders (primary CoQ10 deficiency and trichohepatoenteric syndrome respectively). Multiple splice-disrupting variants in TYRO3 with varying VAFs (6.5-21.1%) indicate possible somatic mosaicism or tumor heterogeneity, though their clinical significance remains unclear.

Multiple high-confidence oncogenic driver mutations detected across critical cancer genes (ALK, PIK3CA, NRAS, KRAS, EGFR) with consistent VAFs suggesting clonal expansion. The presence of established pathogenic variants in KRAS (including G12V and G12D hotspots), PIK3CA H1047R, and EGFR exon 19 deletions indicates an aggressive tumor with multiple actionable targets for targeted therapy selection.
